title: Golden Velvety Peruvian Cornmeal Picarones
description: A kid-friendly Peruvian dish made with fine yellow cornmeal and rest-meat, this paleo-friendly picarone is sour and velvety in texture. It is a delicious and healthy snack for all ages.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the cornmeal, rest-meat, sour cream, eggs, salt, baking soda, and anise seeds, mixing until a smooth dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it is pliable and velvety.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and shape each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk shape and place on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Brush the tops of the picarones with coconut oil and drizzle with honey.
Bake the picarones for 20 minutes, or until they are golden brown.
Remove the picarones from the oven and let them cool for 5 minutes before serving.
